title: pimelea simplex subsp. simplex
intro: Pimelea simplex subsp. simplex, also known as 'Wattles' or 'Rat's Tail', is a shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers in clusters. The plant has been traditionally used in Western Australian Indigenous medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pimelea simplex subsp. simplex include its potential to treat various skin issues, such as acne, eczema, and dermatitis, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Pimelea simplex subsp. simplex can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
